intro: Stenosolen heterophyllus, also known as the Java plum or Jambolan, is a tropical evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its dark green, elliptical leaves and small, white or purple flowers. The Java plum has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, dysentery, and skin conditions, due to its purported health benefits, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stenosolen heterophyllus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as terpenoids and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
